reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Polynomial Hash — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Polynomial Rolling Hash Hash Function Hash functions are used to map large data sets of elements of an arbitrary length ( the keys ) to smaller data sets of elements of a fixed length ( the fingerprints ).

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Bellman Ford — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Bellman–Ford Algorithm The Bellman–Ford algorithm is an algorithm that computes shortest paths from a single source vertex to all of the other vertices in a weighted digraph.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Breadth First Search — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Breadth First Search (BFS) Breadth first search (BFS) is an algorithm for traversing, searching tree, or graph data structures.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Depth First Search — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Depth First Search (DFS) Depth first search (DFS) is an algorithm for traversing or searching tree or graph data structures.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Detect Cycle — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Detect Cycle in Graphs In graph theory, a cycle is a path of edges and vertices wherein a vertex is reachable from itself.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Prim — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Prim's Algorithm In computer science, Prim's algorithm is a greedy algorithm that finds a minimum spanning tree for a weighted undirected graph.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Integer Partition — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Integer Partition In number theory and combinatorics, a partition of a positive integer , also called an integer partition , is a way of writing as a sum of positive integers.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Interpolation Search — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Interpolation Search Interpolation search is an algorithm for searching for a key in an array that has been ordered by numerical values assigned to the keys (key values).

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Knapsack Problem — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Knapsack Problem The knapsack problem or rucksack problem is a problem in combinatorial optimization: Given a set of items, each with a weight and a value, determine the number of each item to include in a collection so that the total weight is less than or equal to a given limit and the total value is as large as possible.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Power Set — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Power Set Power set of a set is the set of all of the subsets of , including the empty set and itself.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Levenshtein Distance — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Levenshtein Distance The Levenshtein distance is a string metric for measuring the difference between two sequences.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: Jump Game — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: Jump Game The Problem Given an array of non negative integers, you are initially positioned at the first index of the array.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Algorithms: N Queens — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: N Queens Problem The eight queens puzzle is the problem of placing eight chess queens on an chessboard so that no two queens threaten each other.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Interview Questions: 36. What is web storage — explain the core idea in your own words.
reviewQuestions.answer: What is web storage Web storage is an API that provides a mechanism by which browsers can store key/value pairs locally within the user's browser, in a much more intuitive fashion than using cookies.

reviewQuestions.question: JavaScript Interview Questions: 465. What is inline caching? — explain the core idea in your own words.
